top_signals.title: China direct strike threat to Australia ‘growing’: report
top_signals.summary: The DF-27 missile has a range of 5,000 to 8,000 kilometers, the U.S. military said in December.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Interview: French Army chief Schill talks technology, surprise and ‘archaic’ combat
top_signals.summary: "We should avoid excessive analogies. France is not Ukraine," said Gen. Pierre Schill in an interview ahead of the Eurosatory defense expo near Paris.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Wall Street and crypto are crashing into each other as tokenized treasury markets hit $14.6 billion
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: SpaceX and OpenAI Are Ending Wall Street’s Era of Stock Scarcity
top_signals.summary: The stock market is starting to expand again after years of shrinking — and potentially at a scale not seen since the dot-com era.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Inside India newsletter: Hollywood is debating AI. India's filmmakers are embracing it
top_signals.summary: Even as Hollywood remains the world's most influential storyteller, experiments in AI filmmaking are increasingly happening in India.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Guidance: Small Business Regulatory Taskforce
top_signals.summary: The Small Business Taskforce is a joint government‑industry group to develop practical recommendations to reduce regulatory burdens on small and medium‑sized enterprises (SMEs) and micro-businesses.

top_signals.title: New Foreign Direct Investment in the United States, 2025
top_signals.summary: Expenditures by foreign direct investors to acquire, establish, or expand U.S. businesses totaled $232.2 billion in 2025, according to preliminary statistics released today by the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis. Expenditures increased $76.8 billion, or 49.5 percent, from 2024 levels. As in previous years, acquisitions of existing U.S. businesses accounted for most of the expenditures. Acquisition expenditures in 2025 were $218.4 billion, expenditures to establish new U.S. businesses were $4.6 billion, and expenditures to expand existing foreign-owned businesses were $9.2 billion. Planned total expenditures, which include both first-year and planned future expenditures, were $284.5 billion. Full Text
